AccessibilityMeasureColorContrast: Sufficient color contrast ratios between text and background
AccessibilityMeasureDarkMode: Dark mode and light mode support respecting OS preferences
AccessibilityMeasureFocusIndicators: Clear focus indicators on all interactive elements for keyboard navigation
AccessibilityMeasureKeyboard: Keyboard navigable interface for all interactive elements
AccessibilityMeasureResponsive: Responsive design that works across devices and screen sizes
AccessibilityMeasureSemantic: Semantic HTML markup for proper screen reader navigation
AccessibilityMeasureTargetSize: Minimum interactive target size of 24x24 CSS pixels
AccessibilityMeasureTextAlternatives: Text alternatives for non-text content where applicable
AccessibilityOurCommitmentDescription: PrTask is committed to ensuring digital accessibility for people with disabilities. We are continually improving the user experience for everyone and applying the relevant accessibility standards.
AccessibilityWcag22ConsistentHelp: 3.2.6 Consistent Help: help mechanisms are in the same relative location across pages
AccessibilityWcag22Description: PrTask targets conformance with the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.2 at Level AA, which includes the following new criteria beyond WCAG 2.1:
AccessibilityWcag22DraggingMovements: 2.5.7 Dragging Movements: alternatives to drag-and-drop interactions are provided
AccessibilityWcag22FocusAppearance: 2.4.13 Focus Appearance: focused elements have a clearly visible focus indicator
AccessibilityWcag22FocusNotObscured: 2.4.11 Focus Not Obscured (Minimum): focused elements are not entirely hidden by other content
AccessibilityWcag22RedundantEntry: 3.3.7 Redundant Entry: users are not required to re-enter information they have already provided
AccessibilityWcag22TargetSize: 2.5.8 Target Size (Minimum): interactive targets are at least 24x24 CSS pixels
BoardDescriptionHeading: Kanban Boards — Visual Task Management and Workflow
BoardDescriptionParagraph1: Boards on PrTask provide a visual, column-based approach to managing development tasks using the Kanban methodology. Each board displays tasks organized into workflow stages — typically columns like To Do, In Progress, In Review, and Done — giving you an at-a-glance view of where every task stands in the development pipeline. This visual representation makes it easy to identify bottlenecks, balance workloads, and ensure that tasks flow smoothly from creation through completion and payment.
BoardDescriptionParagraph2: Creating a board is simple: provide a name and select the board type (Kanban is the default). Once created, you can add tasks to the board and organize them across columns to reflect their current status. Boards support drag-and-drop interaction for moving tasks between stages, making workflow management intuitive and fast. You can create multiple boards for different projects, repositories, or teams, keeping your work organized in ways that make sense for your specific development workflow and organizational structure.
BoardDescriptionParagraph3: PrTask boards sync in real time via WebSocket connections, so changes made by any team member are immediately visible to everyone viewing the board. This real-time collaboration is essential for distributed teams where developers may be working across different time zones. Boards integrate with the PrTask task system, so when a developer submits a pull request for a task on your board, the task status updates automatically. Combined with sprint management and the leaderboard, boards complete the project management toolkit that PrTask provides, enabling teams to plan, execute, track, and celebrate their development work in one unified platform.
BoardSeoDescription: Manage your open source project boards and track pull request tasks. Developers earn money by completing bounty tasks — a perfect side hustle for skilled programmers contributing to open source projects.
CookiePolicyAnalyticsDescription: We may use analytics cookies to understand platform usage and improve the experience. No personal data is shared with third parties for advertising.
CookiePolicyCcpaConsentDescription: Under the California Consumer Privacy Act, PrTask discloses all cookies used and their purposes. PrTask honors Global Privacy Control (GPC) signals as a valid opt-out of any sale or sharing of personal information through cookies. PrTask does not sell personal information collected through cookies.
CookiePolicyConsentDescription: When you first visit PrTask, a cookie consent banner informs you about our use of cookies and allows you to accept all, reject all, or customize your preferences by category. Essential cookies are always active and cannot be disabled. Analytics, functional, and marketing cookies require your consent. You may update your cookie preferences at any time through your account settings or by clearing your browser cookies and revisiting the site.
CookiePolicyContactDescription: For questions about our use of cookies or to exercise your privacy rights related to cookie data, contact our Data Protection Officer at dpo@prtask.com or our privacy team at privacy@prtask.com.
CookiePolicyCsrfPurpose: Protects against cross-site request forgery attacks
CookiePolicyDescriptionParagraph1: This Cookie Policy provides complete transparency about the cookies and similar tracking technologies used on the PrTask platform. We use a minimal set of cookies necessary for platform functionality, user preferences, and anonymous analytics. Each cookie is documented in the reference table above with its name, provider, purpose, duration, and category so you can make informed decisions about your cookie preferences.
CookiePolicyDescriptionParagraph2: PrTask respects your cookie choices in accordance with EU ePrivacy Directive, GDPR, CCPA, and other regional privacy regulations. Essential cookies that are required for platform security and functionality cannot be disabled, but you have full control over functional and analytics cookies. You can manage your cookie preferences at any time through your browser settings or our cookie consent mechanism, and withdraw consent for non-essential cookies without affecting your ability to use the core features of the platform.
CookiePolicyEssentialDescription: PrTask uses essential cookies to maintain your session and authentication state. These are required for the platform to function.
CookiePolicyEuConsentDescription: Under the ePrivacy Directive and GDPR (EU) and PECR (UK), prior opt-in consent is required before setting non-essential cookies. PrTask implements an opt-in model: no non-essential cookies are placed until you explicitly consent. The reject button has equal prominence to the accept button. PrTask does not use cookie walls (denying access for refusing non-essential cookies). Consent is granular per category (analytics, functional, marketing). PrTask maintains auditable records of consent and will request renewal periodically.
CookiePolicyFunctionalDescription: Functional cookies remember your preferences such as theme selection (dark or light mode), language settings, and notification preferences. These cookies enhance your experience but are not strictly required for the platform to operate. You may disable functional cookies without affecting core functionality, but your preferences will not be remembered between sessions.
CookiePolicyGaPurpose: Google Analytics identifier for unique visitor tracking
CookiePolicyIntroduction: This Cookie Policy explains how PrTask uses cookies and similar tracking technologies when you visit or use our platform. It describes what cookies are, the types we use, why we use them, and how you can control or disable them. This policy should be read together with our Privacy Policy.
CookiePolicyManagingDescription: You can disable non-essential cookies through your browser settings. Disabling essential cookies may prevent you from using the platform.
CookiePolicyOtherRegionsDescription: Brazil (LGPD), Thailand (PDPA), South Korea (PIPA), India (DPDP), South Africa (POPIA), and China (PIPL) require consent for non-essential cookies. PrTask applies the opt-in consent model globally to ensure compliance with the strictest applicable requirements. Japan (APPI), Canada (PIPEDA), Singapore (PDPA), and Australia follow notification or implied consent models for non-sensitive cookies, but PrTask provides opt-in consent for all users regardless of jurisdiction.
CookiePolicyPaymentDescription: Payment providers may set cookies during the payment process. These are governed by the respective provider's cookie and privacy policies.
CookiePolicyPrivacyLinkDescription: This Cookie Policy is part of and should be read in conjunction with our Privacy Policy. The Privacy Policy provides comprehensive information about how PrTask collects, uses, and protects your personal data, including data collected through cookies. If there is any conflict between this Cookie Policy and the Privacy Policy, the Privacy Policy prevails.
CookiePolicyRetentionDescription: Essential cookies (session, CSRF) are retained for the duration of your session or up to 30 days. Cookie consent preferences are retained for 1 year. Functional cookies (theme, language) are retained for 1 year. Analytics cookies are retained for up to 2 years. Marketing cookies are retained for up to 3 months. All cookie retention periods are listed in the cookie inventory table above.
CookiePolicyThemePurpose: Remembers your selected theme (dark or light mode)
CookiePolicyThirdPartyDescription: Certain third-party services integrated with PrTask may set their own cookies. These include our payment processor for secure transaction handling and GitHub for OAuth authentication. Third-party cookies are governed by the respective provider's cookie and privacy policies. PrTask does not control these cookies. Third-party cookies are only loaded after you have provided consent where required by applicable law.
CookiePolicyWhatAreDescription: Cookies are small text files stored on your device (computer, tablet, or mobile phone) when you visit a website. They help the site remember your preferences, understand how you use the site, and improve your experience. Cookies can be "persistent" (remaining on your device for a set period) or "session" cookies (deleted when you close your browser). Cookies can be set by the site you visit ("first-party cookies") or by other services that provide content on the page ("third-party cookies").
CookiePolicyWithdrawDescription: You may withdraw your cookie consent at any time by clicking the cookie preferences link in the platform footer, adjusting settings in your account preferences, or clearing all PrTask cookies through your browser settings and revisiting the site. Withdrawing consent does not affect the lawfulness of processing based on consent before withdrawal. After withdrawal, non-essential cookies will be removed and will not be set again until you provide new consent.
CreateTaskDescriptionHeading: Create a Task — Post Bounties for Development Work
CreateTaskDescriptionParagraph1: The Create Task page is where clients post new development work to the PrTask marketplace. Each task requires a clear title, detailed description, linked GitHub repository URL, and a bounty amount that developers will earn upon successful completion. The task creation form guides you through setting all the parameters that help developers understand what needs to be done, how complex the work is, and what they will earn for completing it. Well-defined tasks attract more qualified developers and lead to faster, higher-quality submissions.
CreateTaskDescriptionParagraph2: Complexity levels (Trivial, Simple, Medium, Complex, Expert) help developers gauge the expected effort and skill requirements before committing to a task. Set the complexity honestly to attract the right developers — underestimating complexity may result in incomplete submissions, while overestimating may deter developers from attempting the task. The bounty amount should reflect the complexity and value of the work. PrTask enforces a minimum bounty to ensure meaningful compensation, and developers see the full bounty amount with no hidden deductions. Tags help categorize your task by technology and type for better discoverability.
CreateTaskDescriptionParagraph3: You can also set work item types (Epic, Feature, Story, Task, Bug) to align with your project management methodology, and choose between public and private visibility. Public tasks are visible to all developers on the marketplace, while private tasks are only accessible to invited developers. After submitting a task, it becomes immediately available on the Tasks page where developers can discover and start working on it. Payment for the bounty is processed when you approve and merge the winning pull request, ensuring you only pay for work that meets your requirements and passes your code review.
CreateTaskPaymentHint: No platform fees — developers receive 100% of the bounty.
CreateTaskSubmitAndPayButton: Create Task and Proceed to Payment
CreateTaskVisibilityPrivate: Private - Code changes visible only to participants
CreateTaskVisibilityPublic: Public - Code changes visible to everyone
DmcaCounterNotificationDescription: If you believe your content was removed in error, you may file a counter-notification containing:
DmcaCounterNotificationItem2: Identification of the removed material and its prior location
DmcaCounterNotificationItem3: A statement under penalty of perjury that the material was removed by mistake or misidentification
DmcaCounterNotificationItem4: Your name, address, telephone number, and consent to jurisdiction of the federal court in your district
DmcaCounterProcessDescription: Upon receipt of a valid counter-notification, PrTask will forward it to the original complainant. If the complainant does not file a court action seeking a restraining order against the subscriber within 10 business days, PrTask will restore the removed content within 10 to 14 business days of receiving the counter-notification.
